ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-11-2026 Continued To Jun-22-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Amy Harrington appeared via zoom (former special administrator).

Ms. Harrington expressed her interest in having the final distribution filed, in order to receive payment for her services.

An Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause will be issued to Albert Turnbaugh (attorney) and Adela Sision (executor) to appear on June 22, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to show cause as to why sanctions in the amount of $250.00 should not be imposed against him for failure to file a final distribution and suspending the executor. Status on filing of an fourth account is also continued to that date.



Judge: Michael McNaughton. Clerk: A. Datt. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Warren Woo appeared by zoom for Matthew Terrell (deceased former executor).
Albert Turnbaugh appeared by zoom for Adela Sison (current administrator).

Mr. Woo clarified that the notice of default from the mortgage company was filed in September of 2018, and stated that this is how the beneficiaries realized that the property was in foreclosure and that this information is in paragraph 4 of his petition. Mr. Turnbaugh stated that he thinks his client's objections are valid and that he would like to opportunity to speak with counsel to try to resolve the issues. Discussion was held regarding the reason why Tonya and Ferdinand are not paying rent, Mr. Woo's client having had a signed contract to sell the property prior to his death, Mr. Woo and Mr. Turnbaugh having worked together and sale of the property having closed in January of 2022, the amount of cash that is in the estate, Mr. Turnbaugh's intent to file opposition/objections to the distribution to Ferdinand and to the amount of fees for the former executor, the amount of time each attorney has spent in the case and filing of petitions for extraordinary fees, etc. The court informed Mr. Turnbaugh that he will have to file a petition for approval of report on status of administration prior to the hearing date on status of close. The Petition to Settle First and Final Account and Report of Deceased and Former Executor of the Estate is continued to April 12, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on close of estate is continued from February 27, 2023 to March 27, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The court encourages counsel to meet and confer.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Albert Turnbaugh, in person, for Adela Sison, in person, Petitioner; Amy Harrington, via ZOOM, former Special Administrator; Warren Woo, via ZOOM, for Matthew Terrel, former Executor.

The court queries counsel as to the status of accounting. Ms. Harrington reports that she served as Special Administrator for one month and can file an accounting if requested by the court. The court then advises Mr. Woo that a second accounting on behalf of the deceased Executor is required. ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of The Administration Of The Estate
Off Calendar Jun-15-2021 Continued To Jul-06-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Warren Woo, via ZOOM, counsel for Matthew Terrel, Executor; Amy Harrington, via ZOOM, former Executor.

The court at the outset confirms with Mr. Woo that the Executor has died. Mr. Woo reports that the primary asset of the estate is real property which the decedent had been attempting to sell the property but had been unsuccessful in doing so due to several issues caused by the pandemic and the downturn in the real estate market for occupied real properties. However, the estate has just received a bid for a price that meets the 90% minimum appraised value requirement. Regrettably, Mr. Terrell's untimely death has caused a vacancy of the Executor's position in this matter. Mr. Woo reports that the second person nominated as Executor has advised through her counsel that the plan is for her to file an ex-parte application to be appointed temporary successor-Executor and a petition for appointment as successor Executor.

After additional discussion, the Status of Administration of the Estate is continued to July 6, 2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Aug-31-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Warren Woo, Esq., counsel for Matthew Terrel, personal representative; Albert Turnbaugh, Esq., counsel for Adele Sison, objector.

The Court first questions Mr. Turnbaugh as to the standing of his client to bring the instant petition. Mr. Turnbaugh directs the Court to Probate Code Secs. 1043 and 48, asserting his client's status as the alternate trustee. Mr. Woo responds, noting that the Court has already adjudicated Ms. Sison's status and found it wanting, denying her original petition. Mr. Turnbaugh notes that the fees asserted were nonetheless incurred in the course of probate proceedings even if the petition was denied and alternatively would constitute a creditor claim.

Mr. Woo responds, noting that the attorney fees could not be a creditor claim as these fees are not fees incurred at the behest of or for the benefit of the estate and was not an expense incurred by the decedent prior to death. Mr. Turnbaugh replies.
The Court takes the matter under submission but advises that the Court will adopt the position of Mr. Woo.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-21-2018 Denied

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-21-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Albert Turnbaugh for and with Adela Sison.
Warren Woo for and with Matthew Terrel (Petitioner).
Amy Harrington (Special Administrator) in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that these are competing petitions and that nobody seems to be contesting the will. Counsel indicated that no one has the original will. Ms. Harrington stated that she spoke with Counsel for the lender, that she informed Counsel that the foreclosure notice was ineffective, that the foreclosure is on hold and the mortgage can be brought current, and that she requested a 90-day continuance on the default. Ms. Harrington also stated that she received verbal confirmation of the extension, that she is waiting for written confirmation, and that she found a hard money loan for $70,000.00 at 11% interest with a $3,250.00 initiating fee and a $500.00 processing fee. In addition, Ms. Harrington stated that Ms. Sison offered a loan at 6% based on her appointment and a right of first and last refusal, that Ms. Sison provided a check for $22,320.00 for rents collected. Mr. Woo and Mr. Turnbaugh spoke of delay in filing for probate, the Felton property not being in the Trust, who discovered that the property wasn't in the Trust, Mr. Terrel administering the Trust, the Hidden Valley property having been sold, Ms. Sison taking control of the Felton property and Mr. Terrel being frozen out, mortgage statements being sent to Ms. Sison, rents being sent to Ms. Sison, there being no damage to the estate until Ms. Sison stopped making the mortgage payments, the mortgage company requiring that the payment be increased and not taking a lower payment, the people living in the house not paying any money, Terrel's position to sell the house, etc. Ms. Harrington stated that she thinks the lender will be willing to communicate with who ever gets appointed, but there is at least a five day delay in processing. The Court noted that on the merits everyone agrees that this will is an appropriate will and that it be submitted as a lost will based on the evidence submitted. Ms. Harrington's appointment as Special Administrator is extended until general letters are issued. The Petitions for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary are under submission. The ex parte petition for letters of special administration on calendar for December 19, 2019 is off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

Off Calendar Nov-20-2018 Continued To Dec-18-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Warren Woo for and with Matthew Terrell.
Albert Turnbaugh for and with Adela Sison.
Ferdinand Villanueva (Heir) and Tanya Meuller (occupant at the Felton property) also present.

The Court noted that Ms. Sison's petition is set for December 17, 2018 and that there are competing petitions for special administration on the Monday (11/26/18) ex parte hearing calendar. Mr. Woo raised the issue of foreclosure and stated that the notice of default period is getting really close. Mr. Turnbaugh stated that his Client has obtained funding and that six of the beneficiaries nominated her. The Court encouraged the parties to discuss the matter between now and Monday and try and straighten things out, and not to exhaust estate resources. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to December 18, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The hearing on Ms. Sison's petition on calendar for December 17th is also continued to December 18th.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-06-2018 Continued To Nov-20-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Warren Woo for and with Matthew Terrell (Petitioner).
Albert Turnbaugh for and with Adela Sison.

Mr. Turnbaugh stated that he filed objections, and points and authorities, and that he will need to file his own competing petition. The Court informed Counsel that there was not sufficient time to review the objections and that the matter needs to be continued. Counsel expressed concern about the property going into foreclosure. The Parties were informed that they can file an ex parte petition for special administration to provide authority for the purpose of addressing the foreclosure issue and seeking an extension of the foreclosure deadline. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to November 20, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
